title: FC Bayern München vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC
description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, May 6, 2026 between FC Bayern München and Paris Saint-Germain FC.

markets.question: Will FC Bayern München win on 2026-05-06?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 6, 2026
If FC Bayern München wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the UEFA Champions League semi-final second leg at Allianz Arena, trader consensus favors FC Bayern München at 59.5% implied probability to win, driven by home advantage and Paris Saint-Germain's devastating loss of right-back Achraf Hakimi to a first-leg hamstring tear that sidelines his recovery speed and overlaps against Bayern's wingers. Bayern, trailing 4-5 on aggregate after a thrilling high-scoring opener, boast 42 UCL goals this season and welcome back Raphaël Guerreiro from hamstring issues, offsetting Serge Gnabry's thigh absence. PSG's defensive vulnerabilities exposed without Hakimi drop their odds to 22.5%, while a low 17.5% draw probability reflects the tie's urgency and attacking intent from both sides.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending Champions League holders Paris Saint-Germain host Bayern Munich in the semi-final first leg at Parc des Princes, with mutual injury blows driving tight trader consensus on this high-stakes aggregate tie. Bayern confirmed Serge Gnabry's absence due to a torn right adductor sustained last week, thinning their wing options despite Harry Kane's return from ankle trouble after ousting Real Madrid in quarters; supporting cast includes fit Jamal Musiala and Michael Olise. PSG sweat over midfielder Vitinha's heel inflammation from their recent Ligue 1 defeat to Lyon, plus Nuno Mendes' thigh strain post-Liverpool elimination win, testing Luis Enrique's depth. Historic rivalry and PSG home form favor caution, with lineups pending final checks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the halftime result of this UEFA Champions League semifinal first leg at Parc des Princes as a dead heat across FC Bayern München, Draw, and Paris Saint-Germain FC, underscoring the finely balanced matchup between continental powerhouses. PSG's home advantage and near-clean bill of health—bolstered by Fabián Ruiz's return from knee injury and only Vitinha a heel doubt—counter Bayern's mounting absences, with Serge Gnabry sidelined for the season by an adductor tear, Raphaël Guerreiro out (hamstring), Lennart Karl (hamstring strain), and Tom Bischof (calf) all confirmed unavailable per latest injury reports. Vincent Kompany's suspension adds pressure, but Bayern's domestic momentum and depth like Harry Kane keep the first-half dynamics intensely competitive, favoring a cagey start with no clear edge.

title: FC Bayern München vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC - Exact Score
description: In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game between FC Bayern München and Paris Saint-Germain FC, scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve based on the final score of the FC Bayern München vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC match originally scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET, considering only the result at the end of 90 minutes of regulation plus stoppage time; extra time and penalty shoot-outs are excluded. eventMetadata.context_description: Following Paris Saint-Germain's thrilling 5-4 first-leg semifinal victory over Bayern Munich in the UEFA Champions League, trader consensus reflects extreme uncertainty for the second leg at Allianz Arena, with top exact-score outcomes tightly bunched around 49.5% implied probabilities. Bayern trail by one goal on aggregate and must attack relentlessly at home, leveraging their five straight prior Champions League wins over PSG, but face defensive vulnerabilities exposed by PSG's clinical finishing from Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and others. Bayern's injury woes—Serge Gnabry (thigh, season-ending), Raphaël Guerreiro (hamstring), and Lennart Karl (out)—weaken depth, while PSG remain largely healthy despite Quentin Ndjantou's hamstring absence. Both sides' recent high-scoring form (PSG eight wins in nine, Bayern potent in knockouts) signals another open, goal-filled affair with no clear scoreline favorite.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Bayern Munich hosting Paris Saint-Germain in the UEFA Champions League semi-final second leg on May 6 at Allianz Arena—trailing 4-5 on aggregate after a thrilling first-leg shootout at Parc des Princes—trader consensus on player props hinges on defensive vulnerabilities and attacking firepower. PSG's Achraf Hakimi is sidelined with a hamstring injury from the first leg, exposing their right flank, while Bayern miss Serge Gnabry (season-ending adductor tear) and Raphaël Guerreiro (hamstring), though Alphonso Davies nears return. Harry Kane's scorching form, with 13 Champions League goals this season including recent braces, positions him as a prime anytime scorer amid the home side's urgency to overturn the deficit. High-stakes tension favors overs on shots and goals for key forwards like Kane, Bradley Barcola, and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia.

title: FC Bayern München vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC - Total Corners
description: Total corners markets for the UEFA Champions League game between FC Bayern München and Paris Saint-Germain FC, scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET.

markets.question: FC Bayern München vs. Paris Saint-Germain FC: O/U 12.5 Total Corners
markets.description: In the upcoming UEFA Champions League game between FC Bayern München and Paris Saint-Germain FC, scheduled for May 6, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET: This market will resolve to "Over" if there are 13 or more total corners awarded in this game (combining corners for both teams). If the total number of corners is fewer than 13, this market will resolve to "Under". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50. This market refers only to corners awarded within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time. Corners awarded during extra time or penalty shootouts do not count. The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. eventMetadata.context_description: Trailing 4-5 on aggregate after PSG's thrilling 5-4 first-leg victory, Bayern Munich hosts the Champions League semi-final return leg at Allianz Arena, where home attacking pressure and crowd support could drive elevated corner volume. The opener saw just seven total corners (Bayern 5-2), but both sides average around 5.5-6.5 corners per UCL match this season amid wide play from wingers like Dembélé, Kvaratskhelia, Olise, and Davies. PSG's key right-back Achraf Hakimi is out with a thigh injury sustained in the first leg, weakening overlaps and exposing flanks to Bayern crosses. Expect end-to-end action as Bayern chase advancement, contrasting their Bundesliga average of 9.9 total corners per game.
